data.title: As a DSO user, I get correct ticket state even when the marker and event log drift, because the reducer self-heals orphan markers and the cache key reflects marker state
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add two resilience guarantees to the archived-marker fast-skip implemented in S1a: (1) orphan-marker self-heal — `reduce_all_tickets()` validates marker presence against an `*-ARCHIVED.json` event file in the same dir; if absent (orphan), removes the stale marker and falls back to slow path. (2) Cache-key extension — `compute_dir_hash()` includes `.archived` marker presence in its hash input so marker changes correctly invalidate `.cache.json`.

**Why**: S1a is correct-by-construction under normal lifecycle but can drift under messy git merges, manual tampering, partial-fetch races, or future bugs. Without (1), an orphan marker incorrectly skips an active ticket. Without (2), the per-ticket cache can serve a stale `archived=True` record after self-heal removes the marker, producing split-brain within a single process.

**Scope**:
- IN: Orphan-marker validation in `reduce_all_tickets()` (check for `*-ARCHIVED.json` presence); stale-marker removal on orphan detection; extension of `compute_dir_hash()` to include marker presence/absence; tests covering orphan creation + recovery and cache invalidation across marker changes
- OUT: Any modification to ARCHIVED event writers (handled in S1a); any changes to revert behavior (handled in S1a); CI integration of perf timing (future follow-up)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `reduce_all_tickets()` checks for `*-ARCHIVED.json` event file when it sees an `.archived` marker; if no ARCHIVED event exists, the marker is removed and the reducer falls back to slow path (returning the correct active state)
  ← Satisfies: "`reduce_all_tickets()` validates the `.archived` marker on the fast path by checking for at least one `*-ARCHIVED.json` event file in the same directory. If the marker is present but no ARCHIVED event exists (orphan marker)... the reducer removes the stale marker and falls back to slow path"
- When this story is complete, `compute_dir_hash()` in `ticket_reducer/_cache.py` incorporates `.archived` marker presence/absence into its hash, so any marker change (including self-heal removal) invalidates the per-ticket cache; write an orphan marker, reduce (cache miss, self-heal), reduce again (cache miss due to marker absence), correct active state returned both times
  ← Satisfies: "`compute_dir_hash()` (in `ticket_reducer/_cache.py`) is extended to include `.archived` marker presence/absence in its hash input, so any marker write or removal — including SC 7's self-heal removal — correctly invalidates the per-ticket `.cache.json`"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] This is the drift safety net identified by the concurrency-lens scrutiny pass — without SC 7 and SC 8 working together, cache + fast-skip can disagree within a single process. Test coverage must exercise the cache + stat() + self-heal interaction in sequence, not just each in isolation
- [Testing] Use the repo's existing ticket fixture helpers (`clone_ticket_repo`) to construct orphan-marker scenarios deterministically; no need for real archived tickets
- [Maintainability] `compute_dir_hash()` already exists; the change is additive (add one stat() result to the hash input); document the hash-input format change in a brief inline comment referencing SC 8
- [Performance] Extra stat() per dir during batch reduce is negligible (same order as the SC 1 check and already cached by the OS)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
